product.title: Product
product.description: Managing a small business today is already tough.

meta.title: Cryptographic Lineage for Every Data Asset
meta.description: Forge is an asset materialization engine that produces cryptographic proof of lineage as a natural byproduct of execution. No inference gaps, no metadata sync lag — just verifiable truth baked into every output.

cases.title: Trusted by Platform Teams Building Verifiable Data Infrastructure

features.title: Built for Verifiable Data Pipelines
features.description: Forge replaces fragile metadata layers with cryptographic proofs generated at execution time. Every asset carries its own lineage.

items.title: Cryptographic Lineage
items.description: Every materialized asset is stamped with a cryptographic hash linking it to its source inputs. Lineage isn't inferred — it's produced.

items.title: Concentric Rings Architecture
items.description: Start embedded in a single binary (Ring 0), scale to Kubernetes with PostgreSQL (Ring 2), or run a full HA platform (Ring 3). Same code, same API at every ring.

items.title: Incremental Materialization
items.description: Forge tracks dependency graphs and only re-materializes what has changed. No wasted compute, no stale outputs.

items.title: Native Ecosystem Integration
items.description: First-class support for Arrow, Polars, DataFusion, and Kafka. Embed Forge into existing Rust pipelines without rewrites.

stats.title: Engineered for Production
stats.description: Forge is built for teams that need verifiable data infrastructure without the overhead. Every number here is a design constraint, not a marketing claim.

items.title: Finally, real lineage
items.description: We replaced our entire metadata inference layer with Forge. The cryptographic proofs gave our compliance team something they could actually verify independently.

items.title: From prototype to production in a week
items.description: We started with Ring 0 embedded in a single CLI tool. Two weeks later we were running the same code on Kubernetes. No rewrites.

items.title: Audit-ready from day one
items.description: Our SOX auditors asked for proof of data provenance. We pointed them at the lineage hashes. The conversation was over in minutes, not weeks.

items.title: Lightweight and fast
items.description: The sub-5ms cold start means we can embed Forge in request paths we never thought possible. It changed how we think about materialization.

faq.title: Common Questions, Straight Answers
faq.description: Forge is a new kind of tool — an asset materialization engine with cryptographic lineage baked in. Here's what teams usually want to know before evaluating it.

items.question: How is Forge different from dbt?
items.answer: dbt is a SQL transformation layer that runs inside your warehouse. Forge is a general-purpose asset materialization engine that runs anywhere — embedded in a binary, as a standalone service, or on Kubernetes. Forge produces cryptographic lineage proofs; dbt produces metadata.

items.question: How does this compare to data catalogs like DataHub or Atlan?
items.answer: Data catalogs like DataHub or Atlan observe your systems and infer lineage after the fact. Forge is the execution engine — it produces lineage as a natural byproduct of materialization. There's no inference gap, no metadata sync lag, and the lineage is independently verifiable.

items.question: Do I need to rewrite my entire pipeline?
items.answer: No. Forge is a library, not a platform. You can adopt it incrementally — start with a single asset, embed it in an existing Rust binary, and expand from there. It integrates with Arrow, Polars, DataFusion, and Kafka.

items.question: What compliance standards does this support?
items.answer: Forge's cryptographic lineage provides the verifiable audit trail needed for SOX, GDPR data subject access requests, and HIPAA data provenance requirements. Every materialized asset can be traced back to its sources with mathematical proof.

items.question: How does it scale?
items.answer: Forge uses a concentric rings architecture. Start embedded in a single binary (Ring 0), grow to a standalone orchestrator with SQLite state (Ring 1), scale to Kubernetes Jobs with PostgreSQL (Ring 2), or run a full HA platform with leader election (Ring 3). Same code, same API at every ring.

cta.title: See the Proof
cta.description: Pick one reporting pipeline. Run it through Forge with lineage enabled. In two weeks, you'll know if you can produce a cryptographic proof of every output — and detect any tampering in between. Your data, your infrastructure, your numbers.

benefits.title: Proof of Concept Scoping

benefits.title: Architecture Review
benefits.description: Understand how Forge fits into your existing stack — which ring to start at, which integrations to enable, and how lineage flows through your dependency graph.

benefits.title: Compliance Mapping
benefits.description: Map Forge's cryptographic lineage capabilities to your specific regulatory requirements, whether SOX, GDPR, HIPAA, or internal audit standards.
